subject: History
chapter: Kings and Chronicles: The Mughal Courts

questions.q: Who was the founder of the Mughal Empire?
questions.explanation: Babur was a Central Asian ruler who laid the foundation of the Mughal Empire in India.

questions.q: What was the name of the book written by Abu'l-Fazl to describe the Mughal court and its institutions?
questions.explanation: The Ain-i-Akbari is a detailed account of the administration, economy, and social structure of the Mughal Empire.

questions.q: Who was the Mughal emperor known for his tolerant and inclusive policies towards non-Muslims?
questions.explanation: Akbar introduced the concept of Sulh-i-Kul, or peace with all, and established the Din-i-Ilahi, a syncretic faith.

questions.q: What was the name of the Mughal emperor who introduced the concept of Nazrana?
questions.explanation: Jahangir introduced the concept of Nazrana, or voluntary tribute, to the Mughal emperor.

questions.q: Who was the Mughal emperor who built the Red Fort in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Red Fort, also known as Lal Qila, as his new capital.

questions.q: What was the name of the Mughal emperor who was defeated by the Marathas?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was defeated by the Marathas at the Battle of Bhima Shankar.

questions.q: Who was the author of the book 'The History of the Mughal Empire'?
questions.explanation: Bernier wrote a book titled 'The History of the Mughal Empire', which provides valuable insights into the Mughal court.

questions.q: What was the name of the Mughal emperor who built the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal as a mausoleum for his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.q: Who was the Mughal emperor who introduced the concept of Zaminadari?
questions.explanation: Akbar introduced the concept of Zaminadari, or revenue farming, to increase revenue.

questions.q: What was the name of the Mughal emperor who was known for his cruel and oppressive policies?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was known for his strict adherence to Islam and his suppression of non-Muslims.

questions.q: Who was the author of the book 'The Travels of Mirza Abu'l Fazl'?
questions.explanation: The book provides valuable insights into the Mughal court and its institutions.

questions.q: What was the name of the Mughal emperor who built the Fatehpur Sikri?
questions.explanation: Akbar built Fatehpur Sikri as his new capital.

questions.q: Who was the Mughal emperor who was defeated by the Marathas at the Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was defeated by the Marathas at the Battle of Panipat.

questions.q: What was the name of the Mughal emperor who introduced the concept of Mansabdari?
questions.explanation: Akbar introduced the concept of Mansabdari, or military organization, to increase military efficiency.

questions.q: Who was the author of the book 'The History of the Mughal Empire of India'?
questions.explanation: Bernier wrote a book titled 'The History of the Mughal Empire of India', which provides valuable insights into the Mughal court.

questions.q: What was the name of the Mughal emperor who built the Jama Masjid in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb built the Jama Masjid in Delhi.

questions.q: Who was the Mughal emperor who introduced the concept of Jagirdari?
questions.explanation: Akbar introduced the concept of Jagirdari, or land grant, to increase revenue.

questions.q: What was the name of the Mughal emperor who was defeated by the Sikhs at the Battle of Sirhind?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was defeated by the Sikhs at the Battle of Sirhind.

questions.q: Who was the author of the book 'The History of the Mughal Empire in India'?
questions.explanation: Bernier wrote a book titled 'The History of the Mughal Empire in India', which provides valuable insights into the Mughal court.

questions.q: What was the name of the Mughal emperor who built the Buland Darwaza?
questions.explanation: Akbar built the Buland Darwaza at Fatehpur Sikri.
